events.payload:
**Arc**: The AI has just finished S5 Compile — generated BGM, configured transitions in project.json, registered all assets, and stated that the only remaining step is for the user to click Export in the viewer. The AI's final summary listed several optional next moves (export, tweak shot 6 colour, adjust BGM, upscale images).

**Before**: The AI had completed all agent-side work and reported it done, listing cosmetic options for the user to act on.

**Event (preceding AI turn — verbatim)**: "全部能在我这边做的都做完了 —— 项目已经走到 Compile 的最后一步，就差你在看片器里点 **Export** 渲 MP4 了（导出是浏览器端跑 `@pneuma-craft/video` 引擎，我这边触发不了）。[…] 剩下你可以动的：\n- 看片器里点 **Export** → 出 MP4，然后就能看成片了。\n- 觉得 shot 6 那缕暖橘还是太弱、想要 v2 的话说一声（其他 7 个我看着都立得住）。\n- BGM 想更安静 / 更靠后才进 / 换个走向，或转场想加减，都可以调。\n- 想要更高清的最终图，可以挑几张重生成到 2K。"

**Event (user direction — verbatim)**: "你现在全是图片吗？ 没有 seedance 做视频？"

**Event classification**: user-initiated-pivot

**After — concrete artifacts**: T221: AI ran a curl probe against `fal-ai/bytedance/seedance/v1/lite/image-to-video` and `fal-ai/bytedance/seedance/v1/pro/image-to-video`, confirming availability. T223: AI explained the static-image-only state and offered Seedance i2v. T224: user confirmed. T237–T250: AI generated Seedance i2v clips for all 8 shots, writing `assets/video/shot-1-v1.mp4` through `assets/video/shot-8-v1.mp4`; video clips replaced image clips in `project.json`; provenance recorded `derive` + `select` edges; timeline retimed from 60s to 33.5s.

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (1) The AI's preceding turn listed only cosmetic execution options (export, shot-6 colour, BGM, upscale) — none of them named Seedance or questioned whether clips were video vs. static; no direct question requesting a decision about generation mode was posed. (2) The user introduced a new concern — that all 8 clips were static images with no motion video — unprompted and directive. (3) Within the following turns the work shifted entirely to Seedance i2v generation across all 8 shots, replacing every image asset in the timeline with an mp4.

**Arc**: The AI has just completed the Seedance i2v pass for all 8 shots and retimed the project to 33.5s. It summarised the work and listed optional next moves (export and watch, re-generate any shot, adjust timing/BGM/narration, re-lock phase flags).

**Before**: The AI reported that all agent-side work was done and invited the user to click Export in the viewer to see the finished film.

**Event (preceding AI turn — verbatim)**: "做完了 —— 现在每一镜都是**真·seedance 视频**，全片 **33.5s / 8 镜**。[…] **你可以**\n- 看片器里点 **Export** → 看 33.5s 的成片。\n- 哪一镜的动作/镜头不对，告诉我，我重生成那一镜的视频（换 seed / 调运动提示 / 上 720p）。\n- 觉得节奏还想调（哪镜长短、转场加减），或旁白要再改，说一声。\n- 满意了就把 skeleton / detail / compile 重新锁上。"

**Event (user direction — verbatim)**: "导出的视频没有任何声音，也没有 transition 效果呀～ 跟预览的完全一样。。"

**Event classification**: user-initiated-pivot

**After — concrete artifacts**: T279–T286: AI inspected `@pneuma-craft/video` and `@pneuma-craft/timeline` package source to understand what the export engine supports natively for audio and transitions. T287: AI ran ffmpeg to bake fade-in (0.5s) into `shot-1-v1.mp4` and fade-out (0.35s) into `shot-4-v1.mp4`, `shot-5-v1.mp4`, `shot-7-v1.mp4`, `shot-8-v1.mp4`, producing `shot-{1,4,5,7,8}-v1f.mp4`. T289–T290: brightness checks on extracted frames confirmed fades were working. T291–T295: AI updated `project.json` to point clip assetIds at the ffmpeg-processed fade variants and recorded new provenance edges.

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (1) The AI's preceding turn offered vague open-ended options (re-export, re-generate, re-lock) — none of them were a direct question or ≥2 named options about audio/transition export fidelity; the AI had said nothing about the export lacking sound or transitions. (2) The user reported a concrete new problem — exported MP4 had no audio and no transition effects — directive and unprompted; it was not covered by any option the AI had named. (3) Within the next 18 turns the work shifted to investigating the export engine's audio/transition capabilities and baking fade effects directly into video files via ffmpeg, producing a new set of processed clip files registered in project.json.
